svg.text: Repository-grounded application view | React SPA + FastAPI service core + SQLAlchemy persistence + OpenAI-assisted review

g.text: Cedants, TPAs, watchlists, and business consumers
Role-aware React workspace, approvals, dashboards, and operational review
FastAPI routers, service layer, repositories, auth, and execution controls
Implemented cession, settlement, screening, approval, and reporting pipeline
OpenAI-assisted verification, SQL planning, and HITL governance
Relational persistence, JSON workflow state, workbook cache, and generated artifacts
Logging, audit, security, approvals, and delivery realities in the repo

text.tspan: TanStack Query, Axios, Zustand, React Router, Recharts, Lucide, role-based navigation, dashboard, worklist,
underwriting, claims, operations, compliance, reports, admin, and IRiS Assist drawer
SQLite is the default runtime in this
drive async progress. No Redis, Celery,
Agent steps: Detect & Map, Anomaly Detection, Resolution,
Sanction Screening, File Generation | System steps:

g.text: Role-specific dashboards, drawers, tables, detail workspaces, and charts

svg.text: Execution model: FastAPI BackgroundTasks + frontend polling. No external broker or websocket layer is implemented in the repo.
Internal step ids implemented in claims: upload -> detect-map -> validate -> exceptions -> clauses -> process -> summary -> screening -> files -> worklist -> audit
Active AI paths in the codebase: sanctions verification, low-confidence detect/map fallback, and read-only chatbot SQL planning. No LangGraph, vector DB, or RAG store was detected.
JSON stores provide durable workflow state where schema-backed tables do not yet exist.
Diagram reflects the repository state inspected on 2026-05-20. Items shown as JSON/mock overlay are implemented in code but not fully normalized into the relational schema.
